stories.title: Kyle Tucker's Back Scare — Dodgers Lose Star Early, Win Anyway
stories.angle: Los Angeles Dodgers outfielder Kyle Tucker ($240M man) left Monday's game against the Minnesota Twins in the second inning with low back spasms. Catcher Dalton Rushing also exited the same game for concussion testing. The Dodgers won 2-1 anyway. Tucker says he "felt it" in the first inning on defense and called the decision to exit "completely precautionary." He's day-to-day. For the World Series favorites, even a day-to-day designation is a storyline worth watching — particularly with the trade deadline and All-Star break both coming in the next six weeks.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Tucker left Monday's game vs the Twins in the 2nd inning — low back spasms.

"Back just lit up," Tucker said afterward.

He's day-to-day. Dodgers won 2-1 anyway. But the injury watch starts now. #Dodgers #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Tucker AND Dalton Rushing both left Monday's Dodgers game with injuries.

Tucker: back spasms (day-to-day)
Rushing: concussion testing

LA still won 2-1. Depth is everything. But you don't want to be rolling the dice this time of year. #Dodgers #MLB

facebook.long_form:
DODGERS INJURY WATCH: KYLE TUCKER EXITS MONDAY'S GAME WITH BACK SPASMS

This is the kind of news no Dodgers fan wants to see — even when the team wins.

Kyle Tucker, the $240 million outfielder the Dodgers brought in to push them over the top, left Monday night's game against the Minnesota Twins in the second inning with low back spasms. He was warming up for defense between innings in the first when he felt it.

"Back just lit up," Tucker told SportsNet LA's Kristen Watson after the game.

The team listed him as day-to-day and described the exit as completely precautionary. Manager Dave Roberts said he wasn't overly concerned. The Dodgers won the game anyway, 2-1.

But Tucker wasn't the only Dodger who left early. Catcher Dalton Rushing also exited Monday's game for concussion testing. Two starters out in the same game, and the World Series favorites still took the W.

The reassuring part: Grade 1 MCL sprains, back spasms, day-to-day designations. These are manageable injuries for a roster this deep. The Dodgers don't panic over these things.

The less reassuring part: it's June. The trade deadline is August 3. The All-Star break is July 14. Tucker is a central piece of a World Series run, and "day-to-day" is always one flare-up away from becoming "10-day IL."

We'll be watching this one closely.
facebook.image_caption:
Tucker is out. Rushing is out. The Dodgers still win 2-1.

Depth is everything — but LA can't afford to play all summer without their $240M man. Day-to-day for now. ⚾ #Dodgers #MLB

article.headline: Kyle Tucker's Back Spasms: Dodgers Lose Star Early, Win Anyway

stories.title: Schwarber at 29 — Can He Really Hit 60?
stories.angle: Kyle Schwarber leads MLB with 29 home runs. His pace projects to 63+ on the season. The 60-home run club has exactly five members in baseball history: Barry Bonds (73), Mark McGwire (70, 65), Sammy Sosa (66, 65, 63), Roger Maris (61), and Babe Ruth (60). Schwarber is chasing that list at Citizens Bank Park — the same park hosting the Home Run Derby on July 14. The stage is set for one of the great individual storylines of the 2026 season.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber has 29 home runs. On pace for 63.

The 60-HR club has 5 members in history: Bonds, McGwire (twice), Sosa (three times), Maris, Ruth.

The Home Run Derby is July 14 at Citizens Bank Park.

That's his house. 🏟️ #Phillies #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber's pace: 63 HRs.

Nobody in Phillies history has ever hit 60 in a season.

He leads MLB with 29 through June 23. Yordan Alvarez is No. 2 with 25.

This is getting serious. 👀 #Phillies #HomeRunDerby

facebook.long_form:
KYLE SCHWARBER IS ON A HOME RUN RAMPAGE — AND THE HISTORY BOOKS ARE IN SIGHT

Let's take stock of where Kyle Schwarber stands right now: 29 home runs, leads all of MLB, and on pace for 63+ on the season.

To put that in perspective: the 60-home run club has exactly five members in the history of baseball.

Barry Bonds hit 73 in 2001. Mark McGwire hit 70 in 1998 and 65 in 1999. Sammy Sosa hit 66 in 1998, 65 in 2001, and 63 in 1999. Roger Maris hit 61 in 1961. Babe Ruth hit 60 in 1927.

Schwarber is chasing a list that includes the most controversial and most revered power hitters in the sport's history.

He's doing it in Philadelphia, at Citizens Bank Park, for the first time since this lineup first started clicking. He's doing it heading toward a Home Run Derby that will be held at Citizens Bank Park on July 14 — his home ballpark, in front of his fans.

The context that makes this even more remarkable: Schwarber set a career-high with 56 home runs last season. He came into 2026 and got better. He's already at 29 heading into late June.

Yordan Alvarez is No. 2 in the home run race at 25. The gap is growing.

At some point in the next few weeks, we may need to stop framing this as "can he do it?" and start treating 60 HRs as a reasonable expectation. That's where this is headed.
facebook.image_caption:
29 HRs. Pace for 63. The Home Run Derby is at Citizens Bank Park on July 14.

Kyle Schwarber is chasing history — and he's doing it at home. 🏟️ #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber at 29: Can He Really Hit 60 Home Runs?

stories.title: MLB Draft Combine Opens Today — 335 Prospects, 7 of Top 10 at Chase Field
stories.angle: The 2026 MLB Draft Combine opens today at Chase Field in Phoenix — the fourth consecutive year at the Diamondbacks' ballpark. 335 prospects will take part: 195 collegiate players and 140 high school athletes. 192 of MLB's top 200 prospects are attending, led by seven of the top 10. The No. 1 prospect is UCLA SS Roch Cholowsky. No. 2 is Texas prep SS Grady Emerson. Top pitcher is UC Santa Barbara RHP Jackson Flora. Live coverage starts on MLB Network at 12 PM ET. In 2025, 82.8% of combine attendees were drafted, including 87 of the first 100 picks.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
The 2026 MLB Draft Combine is underway at Chase Field in Phoenix.

335 prospects. 192 of the top 200 here. Live on MLB Network at noon ET.

No. 1 prospect: UCLA SS Roch Cholowsky.
Top pitcher: Jackson Flora, UC Santa Barbara.

The next gen of MLB starts today. ⚾ #MLBDraft

facebook.long_form:
THE 2026 MLB DRAFT COMBINE IS HAPPENING TODAY — HERE'S WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW

The 2026 MLB Draft Combine opens today at Chase Field in Phoenix, and it's the biggest single showcase event between now and the actual MLB Draft. Here's the quick breakdown:

📍 WHERE: Chase Field, Phoenix (4th straight year)
📅 WHEN: June 23–26
👤 WHO: 335 prospects — 195 college players, 140 high school
📺 HOW TO WATCH: MLB Network starting 12 PM ET; also streaming on MLB.TV and MLB.com

Who are the top names to watch?

🔵 No. 1 OVERALL — Roch Cholowsky, SS, UCLA. The top prospect on Baseball America's BA 500. The kind of shortstop prospect you only see a few times per decade.

🔵 No. 2 OVERALL — Grady Emerson, SS, Texas high school. A prep prospect with a premium defensive profile and plus bat speed.

🔵 TOP PITCHER — Jackson Flora, RHP, UC Santa Barbara. The top pitching prospect in this draft with a power arm and advanced feel for pitching.

The Combine matters because it's the last comprehensive evaluation before the draft. Scouts see all the top players in one place, run the same tests, clock the same 60-yard dashes. Careers are made and draft boards are reshuffled here.

In 2025, 82.8% of Combine attendees were drafted (256 of 309), and 87 of the first 100 picks were Combine participants.

Today's coverage on MLB Network: the future of the sport is on screen at noon ET.
facebook.image_caption:
The 2026 MLB Draft Combine opens TODAY at Chase Field. 335 prospects. 7 of the top 10. Live on MLB Network at noon ET.

Who's your pick at No. 1? 👇 #MLBDraft #Baseball

article.headline: MLB Draft Combine 2026: 335 Prospects, the Top Names, and What to Watch

stories.title: Skubal Deadline Buzz Heats Up — Tigers Want One Elite Prospect
stories.angle: The August 3 deadline is 41 days away and Tarik Skubal trade talks are already dominating the hot stove. The Tigers reportedly want one elite prospect plus two to three lesser prospects in any deal — similar pricing to recent rental ace trades. The Dodgers are considered the favorites to land him. The Yankees, Rays, Padres, and Brewers are also involved. Skubal himself reportedly "warned" the Tigers amid the trade chatter, adding tension to an already volatile situation. Meanwhile, the Red Sox are emerging as a seller: closer Aroldis Chapman is their most likely trade chip.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
The #Skubal trade machine is running hot.

Tigers want: one elite prospect + 2-3 pieces.
Dodgers: favorites.
Also in: Yankees, Rays, Padres, Brewers.

Red Sox counter-offer: Aroldis Chapman available.

Aug. 3 deadline. 41 days. 🔥 #MLB #TradeDeadline

facebook.long_form:
THE SKUBAL DEADLINE COUNTDOWN HAS OFFICIALLY STARTED

The August 3 MLB trade deadline is 41 days away. And the biggest story at this deadline has been clear for months: Tarik Skubal.

The Detroit Tigers' ace is almost certainly going to be traded. The Tigers are sellers. Skubal is a Cy Young Award winner who will be a free agent after this season. The bidding war for a player of his caliber — a rental ace in his prime — is going to be one of the most-watched deadline stories in recent memory.

Here's what we know:

💰 The Tigers' asking price: One elite prospect plus two to three lesser pieces — similar to the pricing on other recent top-tier rental arm trades.

🔵 The frontrunner: The Los Angeles Dodgers. Multiple reports have the Dodgers as the favorites. When the Dodgers want someone, they usually find a way.

🤼 The field: New York Yankees, Tampa Bay Rays, San Diego Padres, and Milwaukee Brewers are also reportedly in on Skubal. That's five legitimate bidders for the best pitcher on the market.

⚠️ The wild card: Skubal reportedly "warned" the Tigers amid trade rumors. The dynamics between a player and a trading team always add unpredictability to these situations.

On the other side of the market: The Boston Red Sox appear to be moving into seller mode. Closer Aroldis Chapman is their most likely trade chip and is being tracked by every contender needing bullpen help.

41 days. Get ready for a busy August 3.
facebook.image_caption:
The Skubal trade machine is running. Tigers want one elite prospect. Dodgers are the frontrunners.

41 days to the August 3 deadline. This is the one to watch. ⚾ #MLBDeadline #MLB

article.headline: Skubal Trade Buzz Heats Up: Tigers Want One Elite Prospect, Dodgers Lead the Race

stories.title: Bobby Witt Jr. — IL Decision Pending, All-Star Hopes on Hold
stories.angle: Bobby Witt Jr. (Grade 1 MCL sprain, right knee) was held out of Sunday's game vs the Cardinals after manager Matt Quatraro called the diagnosis "best case scenario." He was expected to be reevaluated before Monday's Tampa Bay series. He's been day-to-day. With 4.5 fWAR and an AL MVP candidacy, every missed game matters. The All-Star Game is July 14 in Philadelphia. If Witt goes on the 10-day IL today, his return window could overlap with the Midsummer Classic.

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
Bobby Witt Jr. (Grade 1 MCL sprain) was supposed to be reevaluated Monday before the Tampa Bay series.

4.5 fWAR. AL MVP candidate. 26 years old.

All-Star voting closes Thursday. The Royals haven't confirmed his status yet.

The clock is ticking. ⏱️ #Royals #MLB

facebook.long_form:
WHERE IS BOBBY WITT JR.?

Royals fans are asking the question that matters most right now: will their franchise player be on the IL when rosters are sorted out today?

Bobby Witt Jr. — 26 years old, the AL's most exciting young shortstop, and the front-runner in the AL MVP race with 4.5 fWAR — has been dealing with a Grade 1 MCL sprain in his right knee since sliding for a ball in a game against the St. Louis Cardinals last week.

Manager Matt Quatraro called the diagnosis "best case scenario" for the injury type. MCL sprains come in three grades: Grade 1 is the mildest — a ligament stretch without a tear. The medial meniscus is intact. The typical recovery for a Grade 1 is one to three weeks of limited activity.

Witt was held out of Sunday's game against the Cardinals. The Royals said he'd be reevaluated before Monday's series opener against the Tampa Bay Rays. Maikel Garcia has been covering at shortstop in the interim.

Here's the tension: Phase 1 of MLB All-Star voting closes Thursday, June 25. If Witt can't demonstrate he's healthy this week, the conversation around his All-Star appearance (July 14 in Philadelphia) gets complicated. A 10-day IL stint starting today would have him eligible to return July 3.

For now, the Royals are hoping day-to-day stays day-to-day. The AL MVP race — and the All-Star ballot — are watching.
facebook.image_caption:
Bobby Witt Jr. (Grade 1 MCL, right knee) was expected to be reevaluated Monday before Tampa Bay.

AL MVP candidate. All-Star voting closes Thursday. Every game matters now. ⏱️ #Royals #MLB

article.headline: Bobby Witt Jr.: IL Decision Looms, All-Star Hopes in Limbo
